identity.name: Auto Claude
identity.description: A multi-agent autonomous coding framework that builds software through coordinated AI agent sessions. Uses Claude Code SDK to run agents in isolated workspaces with security controls. Provides both a Python CLI backend and an Electron desktop UI.
identity.tagline: Your AI coding companion. Build features, fix bugs, and ship faster — with autonomous agents that plan, code, and validate for you.

community.description: Free Discord community for support, bug reports, and networking

concept.problem_statement: AI-assisted coding tools require constant human oversight and context switching. Developers spend time manually coordinating between planning, coding, and validation phases instead of focusing on high-level decisions.
target_audience: Teams wanting to parallelize AI coding
concept.value_proposition: 10x developer output by running autonomous agents that handle planning, coding, and validation in isolated git worktrees while maintaining code quality through self-validating QA loops.

architecture.module_organization: Domain-based with clear separation: agents, memory, merge, spec, security, integrations, analysis, prediction, context

backend_modules.agents: Core agent session logic (spec creation, coder, QA, recovery)
backend_modules.analysis: Project analyzers (routes, ports, databases, services, auth, jobs, migrations)
backend_modules.merge: Sophisticated merge (SemanticAnalyzer, ConflictDetector, AutoMerger, AIResolver, FileEvolutionTracker)
backend_modules.security: Validators (Database, Filesystem, Git, Process, ScanSecrets, Hooks)
backend_modules.spec: Spec pipeline (phases, validation, requirements, critique, complexity)
backend_modules.qa: QA loop (reviewer, fixer, loop orchestrator, report)
backend_modules.runners: CLI runners (spec, insights, roadmap, ideation, ai_analyzer)

frontend_services.RateLimitDetector: Rate limit detection (session/weekly) and profile switching

architecture.notes: Clean architecture with facade pattern for backward compatibility. Core logic in agents/ with re-exports. Memory system has dual-layer (file + Graphiti graph). Merge system has sophisticated 3-tier strategy (git auto-merge, conflict-only AI, full-file AI). Prediction system anticipates implementation risks. 7 project analyzers for comprehensive codebase understanding.

frontend.component_library: Radix UI (comprehensive: dialog, dropdown, tabs, tooltip, etc.)

thinking_levels: Ultra Think (extended reasoning for complex tasks)
task_creation_wizard.reference_images: Drag-drop support, up to 10MB each, max 10 images
task_creation_wizard.reference_files: Project file tree browser with drag-to-add

task_complexity_auto_assessment.example: Identified as simple task with 90% confidence
task_complexity_auto_assessment.impact: Dictates spec depth, testing strategy, and resource allocation

features: Individual terminal headers with task context

roadmap_feature.prioritization: MoSCoW (Must Have, Should Have, Could Have, Won't Have)
roadmap_feature.phase_tracking: Feature count per phase with skip tracking

ideation_feature.description: AI analyzes project to identify low-hanging fruits and quick wins

insights_feature.description: ChatGPT-style project conversations with chat history

frontend.notes: Polished, production-grade desktop app comparable to commercial tools like Linear or Notion. Features 5-stage Kanban workflow with task progress bars, color-coded status badges, and time stamps. Task Creation Wizard includes model selection (Claude Opus 4.5), thinking levels (Ultra Think for extended reasoning), reference images via drag-drop (up to 10MB, max 10), reference files browser, and optional human review before coding. Agent Terminals display in 2x3 grid with streaming status badges and real-time output. Roadmap uses MoSCoW prioritization with AI-generated features, impact scoring, and target audience analysis. Phase visualization shows Plan/Code/QA tabs with progress indicators. Clean dark theme with good contrast and subtle gradients. Sidebar navigation includes: Kanban Board, Agent Terminals, Insights, Roadmap, Ideation, Changelog, Context, GitHub Issues, Worktrees. Uses Zustand for state, xterm.js for terminals, node-pty for native terminal support.

authorization.library: Built-in security layer with command allowlisting

rate_limiting.library: Custom handling for Claude API rate limits

backend.notes: Uses Claude Agent SDK (claude-agent-sdk) for LLM interactions. Multi-phase spec creation pipeline with 3-8 phases based on complexity. Sophisticated agent orchestration with planner, coder, QA reviewer, and QA fixer roles.

multiple_claude_accounts.description: Configure multiple Claude Code Max subscriptions
multiple_claude_accounts.benefit: Swap between accounts for continuous background work
multiple_claude_accounts.upcoming: Auto-switching based on rate limit detection

github_issues_integration.description: GitHub issues flow directly into Auto Claude
github_issues_integration.workflow: Create tasks from issues, auto-identify and fix problems in background

coming_soon: BMAD method for context engineering and AI coding validation
Auto account switching based on rate limits

database.notes: Optional graph database (FalkorDB) for cross-session memory via Graphiti. Primary storage is file-based (specs, plans, memory in JSON/Markdown). Docker Compose provided for FalkorDB setup.

extended_thinking.usage: Spec Critic uses extended thinking for self-critique on complex tasks

prediction_system.purpose: Anticipates implementation risks based on historical patterns

project_analyzers.purpose: Comprehensive project understanding for context building

ai_ml.notes: Sophisticated multi-agent system with 15+ agent types. Spec creation uses 3-8 phases based on complexity. Spec Critic uses extended thinking for self-critique. QA loop runs up to 50 iterations with recurring issue detection (3+ occurrences → human escalation). Prediction system anticipates risks. 7 project analyzers provide comprehensive codebase understanding. Supports MCP servers for external tools (Puppeteer, Linear, Context7).
ai_ml.cost_efficiency_claim: Memory system improves with use - smarter context retrieval at smaller token usage over time, potentially making it cheaper than raw Claude Code

realtime.notes: Real-time updates via Electron IPC between main and renderer processes. File system watching with chokidar for spec/task changes.

dependencies.notes: Well-maintained dependencies. Python dependencies are minimal (3 direct). Frontend uses modern stack with React 19, latest Electron. Native module (node-pty) requires platform-specific prebuilds.

code_quality.notes: Pre-commit hooks enforce linting on both Python and TypeScript. Ruff configured with comprehensive rule sets (E, W, F, I, B, C4, UP). Python uses type hints. TypeScript strict mode enabled.

testing.notes: Comprehensive Python test suite (48 test files) covering security, merging, QA, spec creation, and more. Frontend has Vitest setup. E2E via Playwright. Tests run on Python 3.11 and 3.12.

devops.notes: GitHub Actions for CI (lint, test). Docker Compose for FalkorDB only. Native module prebuilds workflow for Windows. Discord webhook for release notifications. Desktop app distributed as standalone Electron build.

three_layer_model.layer1: OS Sandbox - Bash commands run in isolation
three_layer_model.layer2: Filesystem Permissions - Operations restricted to project directory
three_layer_model.layer3: Command Allowlist - Dynamic allowlist based on project stack analysis

security.notes: Three-layer security model with 7 dedicated validator types. Security hooks validate all operations before execution. Agent-type-based tool filtering (QA agents get different tools than Coder). Secrets scanning with .secretsignore support. Security profile cached in .auto-claude-security.json.

performance.notes: Desktop app with efficient IPC. Terminal grid supports up to 12 concurrent agents. Parallel merge resolution for multiple conflict files. File-based caching for specs and memory.

observability.notes: Custom task_logger provides structured logging per spec. No external observability tools. Progress tracked via build-progress.txt and implementation_plan.json.

documentation.notes: Comprehensive README with Docker setup, CLI usage, architecture overview. CONTRIBUTING.md with detailed setup instructions. CLAUDE.md for Claude Code integration. guides/ folder with CLI usage and Docker setup docs.

developer_experience.notes: Pre-commit hooks for linting. pnpm/npm scripts for frontend. Python CLI for backend. Electron dev mode with hot reload. Husky configured for git hooks.

licensing_legal.notes: AGPL-3.0 is copyleft - requires source code disclosure for network services. May limit commercial adoption. Commercial licensing mentioned for closed-source usage.

community.notes: Active development with regular releases (v2.5.0). Discord community linked. Single primary contributor. CONTRIBUTING.md with detailed guidelines.

strengths: Sophisticated multi-agent architecture with 15+ agent types across 4 categories (spec, implementation, QA, analysis)
Three-layer security model with 7 dedicated validator types (Database, Filesystem, Git, Process, Secrets, Hooks, Registry)
Git worktree isolation for safe parallel development with 3-tier merge strategy
AI-powered merge conflict resolution with 98% prompt reduction via conflict-only prompting
Cross-session memory via Graphiti graph database with cost efficiency over time
Extended thinking integration for spec self-critique on complex tasks
Prediction system that anticipates implementation risks based on historical patterns
7 project analyzers for comprehensive codebase understanding (routes, ports, databases, services, auth, jobs, migrations)
QA loop with up to 50 iterations and recurring issue detection (3+ → human escalation)
Comprehensive test suite (48 Python test files)
Production-grade Electron UI with 15+ Zustand stores, comparable to Linear/Notion
Well-documented with excellent README and CONTRIBUTING guides
weaknesses: AGPL-3.0 license may limit commercial adoption
Heavy dependency on Claude Code CLI and subscription
No dedicated API documentation or OpenAPI spec
No official releases/versioning visible on GitHub
opportunities: Add support for more LLM providers (Gemini, Mistral)
Create VS Code extension for integrated experience
Add web-based interface alternative to Electron
Create plugin/extension system for custom agents
threats: Rapid evolution of AI coding landscape (new competitors)
Dependency on Claude Code SDK (single vendor)
AGPL licensing may deter enterprise adoption
Complex setup requirements may limit adoption
summary.recommendation: Auto Claude is an impressive, well-architected autonomous coding framework that demonstrates sophisticated AI agent orchestration. The multi-agent pipeline with self-validating QA loops is particularly innovative. The codebase shows good engineering practices with comprehensive testing and proper separation of concerns. However, the AGPL license, single-contributor status, and Claude Code dependency are concerns for production adoption. Suitable for individual developers and teams comfortable with copyleft licensing who want to maximize AI-assisted coding productivity.
key_differentiators: 15+ specialized agent types across 4 categories (spec creation, implementation, QA, analysis)
Self-validating QA loop with up to 50 iterations and recurring issue detection (3+ → human escalation)
Extended thinking integration for spec self-critique on complex tasks
Prediction system that anticipates implementation risks based on historical patterns
7 project analyzers (routes, ports, databases, services, auth, jobs, migrations)
3-tier merge strategy: Git auto-merge → Conflict-only AI (~98% prompt reduction) → Full-file AI
Git worktree isolation for parallel agent work
Graph-based cross-session memory with cost efficiency over time
Dynamic complexity-based spec creation (3-8 phases) with confidence scoring
3-tier complexity model: SIMPLE (3 phases), STANDARD (6 phases), COMPLEX (8 phases)
7 security validators (Database, Filesystem, Git, Process, Secrets, Hooks, Registry)
Desktop UI with 15+ Zustand stores and up to 12 concurrent terminals (10-day session history)
Task Creation Wizard with model/thinking level selection and reference images
AI-generated product roadmap with competitor analysis and MoSCoW prioritization
Multiple Claude account support with rate limit detection (session/weekly) and profile switching
GitHub Issues integration for automated bug fixing
One-click GitHub release creation from changelogs
Full CLI interface with spec, build, insights, roadmap, and ideation runners
areas_needing_verification: Memory consumption with many concurrent terminals
